title: No Warning When Using Unbased Unsized Literal in Verilog
body:
Hello, I noticed that Verilator does not warn when a SystemVerilog unsized literal (`'0`, `'1` `'x`, `'z`) is used in a Verilog file. (Vivado will correctly give an error in this case)

For example, no error/warning is given on:

```verilog
wire [7:0] a = '0;
wire [7:0] b = '1;
wire [7:0] c = 'x;
wire [7:0] d = 'z;
```

Lint commond
```bash
verilator --lint-only -Wall --language 1364-2005 <file.v>
```

Verilator version
```plain
Verilator 5.007 devel rev v5.006-7-g4a8cfe367
```

I can try to solve this on my own, but I have not looked much at the Verilator source code. Could someone direct me to which file(s) I should be editing?
